bead.title: Story 16.3d: Bring internal/agent test package back under the 600s timeout
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The full-repo `cd cli && go test ./...` gate (parent AC 4) fails because the `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent` package exceeds the 600s test timeout and is killed before completing, so no agent-package result is produced.

ROOT CAUSE
- `go test ./internal/agent/...` accumulates past the 600s budget. The heavy integration/concurrency tests in this package are the likely contributors — e.g. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go:369 `TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded` and the concurrent-attempt suite in cli/internal/agent/work_concurrent_attempts_test.go — whose combined wall time pushes the package over the limit.

PROPOSED FIX
- Profile the slowest tests in internal/agent (use the test-timing path: `go test -json` and the cli/tools/test-timing summary) and reduce wall time on the hot offenders — shrink fixture sizes, parallelize independent cases with t.Parallel where safe, replace real sleeps/polls with deterministic synchronization — so the package completes within the 600s budget without skipping coverage.
- Do not delete or `t.Skip` tests to beat the clock; reduce their cost.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend e2e, spec-doc, and audit-event work (sibling/parent beads).
- The server GetBeadSnapshot and graphql efficacyRows regressions (sibling beads 16.3b / 16.3c).
- New auth or role-based access control.
bead.acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` completes green with no package timeout; cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go:369 `TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded` passes.
2. With siblings 16.3b and 16.3c landed, `cd cli && go test ./...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
